Call 1 of 4. Code Construct 0 and Construct 1 together.
Decision rules for C0:
- Use the highest leaked-information category mentioned in the narrative.
- account_credentials: usernames, passwords, emails, login access.
- personal_identification: name, SSN, date of birth, legal identity data.
- personal_possessions: address, phone number, occupation, physical-world possession markers.
- behavioural_characteristics: habits, preferences, routines, emotional profile.
Decision rules for C1:
- 0 when the narrative does not frame information exposure or ongoing risk at all.
- 2 when the victim takes protective steps like locking cards, changing passwords, or closing accounts.
- 3 when the victim describes sustained risk, bureau freezes, identity-theft reports, or persistent exposure.
- 4 when there are cascading identity consequences or ongoing loss of control despite remediation.
Worked examples:
- Example A: 'I locked all my cards and changed passwords' -> C0 may be account_credentials or personal_identification depending on the breach details, C1=2.
- Example B: 'Someone filed taxes with my SSN and opened accounts in my name' -> C0=2 personal_identification, C1=4 severe_perceived_vulnerability.
Use schema label tokens exactly.

Target narrative:
I filed a Paypal claim against a seller in Massachusetts from whom I purchased an item that was misrepresented to me. When I received the item, I contacted the seller to let her know that the item was not as she described and not acceptable, and that I wished a refund in accordance with her website 's stated refund policy. She promptly blocked me without any response. I made a claim to Paypal, and submitted numerous photos of the item I received along with screenshots of what was advertised, testimonials, and also informed them that the seller blocked me. This was XXXX XXXX. I sent the item back to the seller XXXX XXXX and it was delivered XXXX XXXX, and I provided proof of this. I expected a refund at that point. 

In the meantime, Paypal has continued to make repeated demands for photos, proof and tracking information, which I have provided multiple times. They also demanded at least three times, after I informed them the item was returned to the seller, that I provide an evaluation of the item from an independent party showing the item was not as described. And all this has been done under the threat that if I do not provide this information, they will decide the case in favor of the seller. 

First of all, as the buyer, I am the one who decides if an item is acceptable to me or not, as is my right as a customer. Secondly, I no longer have the item in my possession to bring to any independent party to make such an evaluation. I contacted Paypal last week, and the agent removed the request for an evaluation. They once again made a request for an independent evaluation this past week. I again contacted them and told them the item has been returned to the seller and delivered. I did attempt to get a third-party authenticator to at least look at the photos and try to render an opinion, but they informed me they were unable to do such an evaluation, and I have provided that communiqu to Paypal. And today, for a third time, Paypal asked for tracking information and for me to provide a third-party evaluation. 

There should be no more demands or discussion and they should just issue a refund at this point of the {$1500.00} I paid for an item that was not as it was advertised and not acceptable to me and has been returned. It is not as if I have retained the item in my possession and am demanding a refund. I would add that in addition to all the photos I took and provided to them, I also have video footage of the unboxing of the item in question and the repacking of it for shipment, which I have repeatedly offered to provide Paypal, but never received any answer from them. Instead of refunding, Paypal is being obstinate, hostile and unreasonable. Where is their buyer protection that they tout? This is also false advertising on their part. 

I would appreciate it if there is something you can do to help me recover my funds or can intervene in this matter because Paypal 's conduct here is egregious. 

Thank you in advance.

- 'I lost my retirement savings' -> absolute_code may be 1 or 2 depending on amount visibility, relative=4 life_altering_loss.

I filed a Paypal claim against a seller in Massachusetts from whom I purchased an item that was misrepresented to me. When I received the item, I contacted the seller to let her know that the item was not as she described and not acceptable, and that I wished a refund in accordance with her website 's stated refund policy. She promptly blocked me without any response. I made a claim to Paypal, and submitted numerous photos of the item I received along with screenshots of what was advertised, testimonials, and also informed them that the seller blocked me. This was XXXX XXXX. I sent the item back to the seller XXXX XXXX and it was delivered XXXX XXXX, and I provided proof of this. I expected a refund at that point. 

In the meantime, Paypal has continued to make repeated demands for photos, proof and tracking information, which I have provided multiple times. They also demanded at least three times, after I informed them the item was returned to the seller, that I provide an evaluation of the item from an independent party showing the item was not as described. And all this has been done under the threat that if I do not provide this information, they will decide the case in favor of the seller. 

First of all, as the buyer, I am the one who decides if an item is acceptable to me or not, as is my right as a customer. Secondly, I no longer have the item in my possession to bring to any independent party to make such an evaluation. I contacted Paypal last week, and the agent removed the request for an evaluation. They once again made a request for an independent evaluation this past week. I again contacted them and told them the item has been returned to the seller and delivered. I did attempt to get a third-party authenticator to at least look at the photos and try to render an opinion, but they informed me they were unable to do such an evaluation, and I have provided that communiqu to Paypal. And today, for a third time, Paypal asked for tracking information and for me to provide a third-party evaluation. 

There should be no more demands or discussion and they should just issue a refund at this point of the {$1500.00} I paid for an item that was not as it was advertised and not acceptable to me and has been returned. It is not as if I have retained the item in my possession and am demanding a refund. I would add that in addition to all the photos I took and provided to them, I also have video footage of the unboxing of the item in question and the repacking of it for shipment, which I have repeatedly offered to provide Paypal, but never received any answer from them. Instead of refunding, Paypal is being obstinate, hostile and unreasonable. Where is their buyer protection that they tout? This is also false advertising on their part. 

I would appreciate it if there is something you can do to help me recover my funds or can intervene in this matter because Paypal 's conduct here is egregious. 

Thank you in advance.

The person ( who spoke without an accent ) informed me that someone had tried to open an account with my information. I do not remember getting the " extra warning '' that I was sending to a new recipient, etc. 

It should be noted that at this time, I was a few days home from aXX/XX/XXXXXXXX and on major pain killers. The person on the line asked me if I had spent some time in the last week on public wifi, which I had in the hospital ( I believe they knew I had recently gotten out of the hospital ). 

I should have realized this was a scam immediately, but with the pain meds and the fact that I had indeed just spent much time on public wifi, I didn't catch it. But I realized as soon as I hung up. 

I went immediately to my bank ( Citizens ). They told me they would look into it but didn't think they would be able to help. I also filed a police report, about which I have heard nothing. I got a letter from Citizens stating that I had been denied and they would not return any of the money. 

I called, waited on hold for over and hour to speak to a " manager '' who rudely told me that I had authorized the transfer and despite the way the scammers played on me and despite my highly medicated state, they would re-evaluate, but again they didn't think they could help me. They said they would send me another letter with their decision, which they never did. I have heard nothing since and have given up on trying to recover any money. I am REALLY hoping you can help. Thank you.
